stories.headline: US Adds Fewer Jobs Than Expected in June, Unemployment Rate Falls
stories.summary: US hiring slowed sharply in June as nonfarm payrolls increased 57,000 last month after downward revisions to the prior two months, according to Bureau of Labor Statistics data out Thursday. Michael McKee reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

One possible explanation is that the nutrients in formula remain largely fixed, while breast milk adapts to a baby's changing nutritional needs.
